action_text: Hover over the smallest bar near the right end of the chart to show its tooltip
rationale: We need to read the exact count for the smallest bar to determine the minimum; hovering the tiniest bar (far right among named agents) should show a tooltip with the agent name and count.

action_text: Hover over the next-smallest bar near the right end of the chart
rationale: If the first hover doesn’t reveal a tooltip, try the adjacent small bar (likely another low-count agent) to capture its exact count and name.

action_text: Hover over a third small bar near the right end of the chart
rationale: Hover another nearby small bar to compare counts and confirm which is the minimum across agents.
Using the chart’s options menu may provide a “Show data” or table view, which would list agent names and counts explicitly, making it easy to find the minimum.

action_text: Hover over a mid-small bar in the chart to view its tooltip
rationale: Hover a slightly taller small bar to understand the distribution and ensure we correctly identify the minimum value.
